name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: Are hospitals overwhelmed? "Referring to actual beds, we have them. The limiting factor is healthcare providers, esp. high-level ICU care w/only so many providers to go around...We all need to really think about what more we can do as individuals to limit transmission." -Dr. Dunn

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: "We need to change mindsets about what's reasonable activity during a pandemic, esp. when hospitals are overwhelmed. To get our case counts down, we need to only socialize w/people in our households, wear face coverings when we're out & stay home when we're sick." -@DrAngelaCDunn

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: It was a mental hurdle to start wearing a mask to the grocery store and out in public. The mental hurdle we all need to cross is wearing a mask around our loved ones too-- that's where most of the new cases are coming from.

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: What do you want businesses to know?  "Most new cases are not from people engaging in commerce or people going to work. It's people not practicing those same safe behaviors outside of work that they are when they are at work." -@DerekMillerUtah

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: Does wearing a mask and keeping six feet make a difference?  "Masks and staying away from people are the best ways to stop transmissions, especially as we hear about cases being transmitted by those with no symptoms." - @DrAngelaCDunn

tweet: Q: How can we improve "school at home" both now and after?  A: Transitioning to a heavier technology emphasis with online learning-- and improving access to internet.   Check out my rural broadband bill below ⤵️  https://t.co/gZ9iA6gKFA

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: Q: What will the third wave of legislation include?   A: We hope to include things that won't add to the national debt, such as banking regulations, that can provide resources for businesses but won't add to the deficit.

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: Q: Do masks really work and why do they matter?  A: Masks cut down the transmission of the disease. We'd like to create a culture in Utah where wearing a mask is a responsible, civic token of helping your neighbors and helping yourself.

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: Q: What's the benefit of quarantining the community vs just those in high risk?   A: If everyone were exposed, many more get sick and we'd eclipse our healthcare capacity. This gave us more space to get ahold of PPE (masks and gowns).

tweet: Individuals who filed taxes in '18 or '19 can expect checks directly deposited next week. The @USTreasury has finalized their website allowing non-filers to provide their info to the IRS to receive their checks.   More info here:  https://t.co/k9wqTkcR6z

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: Didn't get the chance to ask a question? My office number is: 202-225-7751. Leave a voicemail and we'll work on getting you an answer. Thanks!

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: The @USTreasury can only print 5 million checks a week so it could take months. Do everything in your power to go the electronic route. Paper checks are unfortunately easier for someone to financially fraud.

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: Q: What's the timeline for the individual payments?  A: We'll start seeing those direct deposits within the next week. If you didn't file taxes in 2018 or 2019, you can submit your banking info through an online portal the @USTreasury is building (or file your taxes now!).

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: You can also follow along on our Facebook Live video here:    https://t.co/0MeBjbz5fL

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: You can also use this website to determine your current risk and provide the @UtahDepOfHealth and government leaders insight into our collective health:  With or without symptoms, your insights will help!   https://t.co/65u88Bi7lF

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: Q: Are there enough tests in Utah?  A: Right now, Utah has the capacity to test anyone with symptoms consistent with COVID19. You can and should be tested.

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: Q: Why are medical masks so hard to come by?  A: The supply doesn't meet the demand. We were not prepared from a supply chain standpoint & now the entire world needs them.   🚨The @CDCgov & @GovHerbert recommend cloth masks for the public; N95 masks are for medical professionals.

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: Q: Will the warmer weather bring #COVID19 relief?  A: No data to support either way. Influenza is seasonal in some areas but constant in tropical regions. Just because it's warming up doesn't mean we'll see relief. This is a novel virus-- we don't know how it'll be affected.

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: Many Q's about EIDL loans. @SBAgov is working directly w/businesses to issue these loans & direct deposit the funds into accounts. With specific issues, submit an inquiry to the Rapid Response Team, you'll hear back between 12-24 hours:   Link here:  https://t.co/kL6PI3YEm0

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: Q: When will we get back to normal life?   A: First we need to get over the peak, and then wait at least two incubation periods (about a month total) and then start slowly going back into normal life. It won't be a light switch flipped back on.

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: Q: Could those who test negative go back to work to help the economy?  A: No because testing negative means you don't have the virus that day, not that you're immune. There's no choice between the economy or lives: if we let people die, the economy is destroyed for a lot longer.

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: Q: How is this different from SARS?  A: SARS was lethal but didn’t spread with the same efficacy. COVID19 is spread more easily, by coughing, sneezing and droplets, plus 80% with this virus are walking around with a mild illness able to spread it unknowingly.

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: Q: Could parts of the country start moving again? Will normal life return all at once?   A: Depends on us. Each city/state has the power to control the scope with social distancing. We're pretty well ahead of the curve in Utah for preventing/slowing the spread- let's stick w/it!

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: Q: Why do we social distance? Why not carry about our normal lives?  A: If we let the disease move unchecked, we'll have far too many cases than hospitals can handle.  By keeping the wave down, we have time to develop drugs to treat this and eventually an effective vaccine.

name: Rep. John Curtis
tweet: On adequate testing in rural Utah:   Unless you are having difficulties breathing, there's no point in getting a test because there is no medicine to cure it yet. Stay home and take care of yourself. If you develop complications, the hospital will be ready to take care of you.
